mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns do not significantly increase when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and lower corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with falling industrial production indicates a shift from goods to services demand, which compresses manufacturing margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 5-day returns more often than the baseline 34.1% negative rate.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts during periods of low inflation signal robust consumer confidence and construction sector earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL is below 195, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate demand and supports corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average, supply-side contraction signals impending economic slowdown and reduced equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite industrial production crossing below its trend.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and federal funds rates indicate aggressive monetary tightening that suppresses risk appetite and equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined inflation and rate hike signal does not produce negative 5-day returns more frequently than the historical baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions signals peak economic activity before a downturn, which historically precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets price in future recessions.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% during late-cycle regimes.
